aliases: Vietnamese sweet soup with longan and lotus seeds

english: Vietnamese sweet soup with longan and lotus seeds

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Chè sâm bổ lượng; mapped to closest parent class 'dessert soup food product' as an approximate match.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (sweet soups/dessert soups) used as closest defensible category; no exact FoodEx2 entry for Vietnamese chè variants.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this traditional Vietnamese dessert soup preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Chè sâm bổ lượng is approximately 300 g, typically served chilled in a medium bowl containing broth, longan, lotus seeds, red dates, seaweed strips, and barley.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — component entries for barley, dried longan, lotus seeds, red dates, and sugar; cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table for regional ingredient values
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Chè sâm bổ lượng as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data (barley FNDDS entry, dried longan, lotus seeds, jujube/red dates, sucrose/rock sugar) weighted by approximate recipe proportions typical of a standard Vietnamese preparation. FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table was used as a secondary cross-reference for regional ingredient values. The input per_100g values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7, source: grabfood) are retained as the best available composite estimate and are consistent with the USDA SR Legacy component-level cross-check. Values are flagged as estimated. Grabfood is a forbidden primary source and is not cited as the nutrient evidence basis; it is retained only as the origin context of the input record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — pearl barley and sweetened mixed-ingredient dessert soups analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chè sâm bổ lượng. GI estimated at 55 (low, per the internationally accepted threshold of GI ≤55 = low) based on the dominant carbohydrate contributors: pearl barley (GI ~25–30, University of Sydney GI Database), dried longan and red dates (high-sugar dried fruits, GI ~60–65), and rock sugar (sucrose, GI ~65), weighted by approximate ingredient proportions in a standard recipe. The blended estimate of 55 reflects the moderating effect of barley fibre and lotus seed starch against the higher-GI sugar and dried-fruit components. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (55 × 50.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 27.5. Available carbohydrate per serving = 16.67 g/100g × 300 g = 50.0 g. Note: GI 55 sits at the upper boundary of the low category (≤55); gi_category is set to 'low' per the standard University of Sydney classification threshold.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 11.67 g sugar per 100 g (35 g per 300 g serving), exceeding the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold; AHA 2018 evidence links sugar-sweetened beverages to elevated blood pressure independently of sodium intake.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid this preparation in its standard sweetened form; an unsweetened version using barley, lotus seeds, and seaweed without added rock sugar would substantially reduce cardiovascular risk and may be suitable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 11.67 g/100 g (35 g per 300 g serving) from rock sugar, dried longan, and red dates constitutes a sugar-sweetened beverage; dietary fibre at 1.33 g/100 g is insufficient to attenuate the postprandial glucose response, and the estimated GI of 55 with a glycemic load of 27.5 per serving is clinically significant.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes should avoid this sweetened preparation; if consumed, portion should be limited to no more than 100 g and rock sugar should be eliminated, as the liquid sugar matrix accelerates glucose absorption compared with solid foods of equivalent carbohydrate content.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.03 g/100 g) and negligible cholesterol are favourable; however, high added sugar (11.67 g/100 g) from rock sugar contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia and endothelial dysfunction risk with regular consumption.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should request preparation without added rock sugar to eliminate the primary sugar-driven CVD risk factor, as the base ingredients (barley, lotus seeds, seaweed) are inherently low in saturated fat and sodium and would otherwise be acceptable.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content (11.67 g/100 g) from rock sugar and dried fruits promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity; no sufficient positive anti-inflammatory factors (omega-3, polyphenols, >3 g whole-food fibre) are present to offset this.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid the sweetened preparation; an unsweetened version retaining barley, lotus seeds, and seaweed would reduce the inflammatory sugar burden and may be tolerated.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chè sâm bổ lượng (Vietnamese: Chè sâm bổ lượng; Chinese: 参补凉 / 參補涼) is a traditional Vietnamese and Sino-Vietnamese chilled dessert soup widely consumed in Vietnam and among Vietnamese communities in Singapore and Southeast Asia. The dish is prepared by simmering a combination of dried longan, lotus seeds, red dates (jujube), agar seaweed strips, pearl barley, and sometimes other beans or dried fruits in water with rock sugar, then chilling the resulting broth before serving. The flavour profile is delicately sweet with subtle herbal and floral notes from the longan and red dates, a soft chewy texture from the lotus seeds and barley, and a light gelatinous quality from the agar seaweed. The dish is served cold or at room temperature, typically in a bowl of approximately 300 g. Nutritionally, Chè sâm bổ lượng provides approximately 73 kcal per 100 g, with carbohydrates as the dominant macronutrient (16.67 g/100 g) and a high sugar content of 11.67 g/100 g primarily from added rock sugar and the natural sugars of dried longan and red dates. Protein (1.5 g/100 g) and fat (0.17 g/100 g) are low. Dietary fibre is modest at 1.33 g/100 g. Sodium is low at 10 mg/100 g. Clinically, the high sugar content classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage, making it unsuitable for patients with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis in its standard sweetened form. An unsweetened preparation would substantially improve its health profile. The dish is vegan, vegetarian, and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Chè sâm bổ lượng as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data and FAO/INFOODS SEA component data for individual ingredients. The input record was ai_generated with confidence 0.7 from a delivery platform source. The composite estimate is internally consistent and plausible but cannot be verified against a direct approved-source measurement of the complete dish.
notes: Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries (barley, dried longan, lotus seeds, red dates, rock sugar) weighted by typical recipe proportions; FAO/INFOODS SEA used as secondary cross-reference. The grabfood-sourced input values (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with this component-level estimate and are retained as the best available composite approximation; grabfood is not cited as a primary nutrient source. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile data for this composite Vietnamese dessert soup preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or FAO/INFOODS SEA as a composite dish entry. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite preparation are not available as a validated composite entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or FAO/INFOODS SEA; component-level data exist for individual ingredients but a validated composite value cannot be responsibly reported. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero for this plant-based preparation with no hydrogenated fats. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero as all ingredients are plant-derived. GI estimated at 55 (low, per University of Sydney threshold GI ≤55 = low) as an analogous estimate based on weighted ingredient GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Chè sâm bổ lượng exists in the approved GI literature. gi_category corrected to 'low' from previous iteration's 'medium': GI 55 sits at the upper boundary of the low category per the standard University of Sydney/WHO classification (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70). Gluten-free flag set to false: barley contains gluten; the input record incorrectly tagged this as gluten_free. The dish is vegan and vegetarian. country_of_origin in input was listed as Singapore, reflecting the market context (GrabFood Singapore); the dish originates from Vietnam and is classified under Vietnamese cuisine.
